subject: Gaming
title: The Legend of Zelda Changes Adventure
description: Learn The Legend of Zelda Changes Adventure in one minute.

hook.text: Miyamoto wanted to recreate the wonder he felt exploring caves as a child. The result was Zelda.

buildup.text: Released in 1986, Zelda gave players a battery-backed save feature—play, stop, and return later.

discovery.text: The open world let players choose their own path. No game had offered this freedom on a console before.

twist.text: Players could get hopelessly lost. Miyamoto made that feeling intentional—exploration was the reward.

climax.text: Zelda pioneered open-world design and action-adventure gameplay still used in games today.

punchline.text: Getting lost was the design. Zelda made exploration the game.

quiz.question: What inspired Miyamoto to create The Legend of Zelda?
options: A board game he played in college
